title: load_state() loads ALL unspent notes witnesses including spent-during-session
description: scanner.rs load_state() calls self.store.spendable_notes(0) to rebuild the in-memory witnesses map. spendable_notes() joins notes with witnesses WHERE spent=0. If a payment is sent (notes marked spent via mark_notes_spent) between process restarts, those notes correctly disappear from the next load_state() call — that part is fine. However, if mark_notes_spent fails after broadcast (the MarkSpentFailed path in payment.rs), the notes remain spent=0 in the DB but their nullifiers are on-chain. load_state() will reload those notes into witnesses and the scanner will continue tracking them as live witness candidates. The witnesses table rows for those notes are never deleted. Combined with nie-en5f.2 (witnesses not purged on spend during a running session), this means the witnesses map can accumulate zombie entries from two sources: (1) notes spent during the running session, (2) notes whose mark_notes_spent failed. Trigger: mark_notes_spent failure after successful broadcast. Bad behavior: zombie notes persist in witnesses map across restarts; double-spend attempt on next send_payment.
